# TikTok Integrity and Authenticity

> **Use boundary:** This file is written for research, ethics, detection, and policy analysis. It describes mechanisms at a conceptual level so they can be studied, criticised, or detected. It is not an execution guide for manipulating platforms, audiences, investors, advertisers, or creators.


## Why this source matters

This source helps draw the boundary between legitimate growth and prohibited or deceptive signal manipulation. Use it to support claims about platform rules, consumer-protection obligations, or enforcement posture.

## What it supports

- The relevant platform/regulator treats authenticity, disclosure, or genuine user experience as important.
- Artificially inflated metrics, fake reviews, hidden material connections, fake accounts, or manipulation of ranking/engagement signals are not treated as harmless optimization.
- The policy/enforcement source gives language for the legal/platform boundary.

## How to use in the paper

Use it in the law/governance section and cross-reference specific case studies. Avoid treating platform policy as law unless it is a regulator/legal source.

## Source notes

- **TikTok Community Guidelines — Integrity and Authenticity** (Platform policy / primary). Bans fake engagement, trade/marketing of engagement-inflation services, covert influence operations, bulk account operation, manipulation of engagement signals, fake personas and spam. URL: https://www.tiktok.com/community-guidelines/en/integrity-authenticity/
